Football.title: Europe’s biggest stadium in contention to host World Cup 2030 final but faces battle with Real Madrid’s Bernabeu
Football.content:
EUROPE’S biggest stadium is in contention to host the World Cup 2030 Final.

It is reported that Barcelona’s iconic Nou Camp venue is one of those under consideration to host the biggest match in football – but they face a battle with rivals Real Madrid and their Bernabeu home.





Reports in Spain claim a FIFA delegation will begin detailed inspection tours of venues across Spain and Portugal next week.

One of those key stops will be the the Spotify Nou Camp – where they will check up on the progress.

Sport.es says that trip to Barcelona’s home will come on March 9.

The visit also includes stops in Zaragoza, Madrid and the likes of Las Palmas, Seville, A Coruna, Bilbao and San Sebastian.

Barcelona moved back into the Nou Camp in November, after months of delays.

But the £1.3billion renovation project is still yet to be completed, which means they are playing in front of a 45,000 reduced capacity.

That could reportedly change when Sevilla come calling on March 15, when an additional 17,000 fans are set to attend.



Barcelona City Council will have to grant the club a 1C license to approve the increase in fans heading through the doors, with club officials expected to be granted the green light.

Eddie Howe and Newcastle will be the first English team to play at the newly renovated Nou Camp.

BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

The Toon Army face the La Liga giants in the last 16 of the Champions League with the first leg next midweek at St James’ Park.

The second showdown between the pair takes place on March 18, three days after that Sevilla outing.

That means there could be 62,000 spectators in the ground for the visit of Eddie Howe‘s side.



But first Barcelona will need to have the approval of Uefa to open up more seats.

The Nou Camp will boast a huge 105,000 spectators when work is completely fully on the iconic arena.

Football.title: Gareth Bale reveals he hid severe injury his entire career and was warned he could tear muscles just ‘brushing teeth’
Football.content:
GARETH BALE feared he would suffer an injury when BRUSHING HIS TEETH after a torn disc in his back plagued his career.

The Welsh superstar, 36, enjoyed a trophy-laden spell with Real Madrid after becoming a Premier League legend with Tottenham.





But Bale announced his shock retirement at the age of 33, after a brief stint with MLS side Los Angeles FC, and his final appearance came at the 2022 World Cup.

The five-time Champions League winner has revealed that he hid a severe injury he suffered as a teenager as he looked to make the grade.

And it resulted in Bale sensationally worrying that brushing his teeth could lead to time on the sidelines.

Speaking on the Stick to Football podcast, brought to you by Sky Bet, he said: “I actually tore my disc in my back when I was 18 at Tottenham. I played my whole career with that.



“I had a lot of calf injuries, which was due to my back. I never came out and said this when I was playing.

“I passed the medical [at Real Madrid] so it was fine. They knew about it. It’s manageable but, over time, it caught up with me.

“I went to see a back specialist, one of the best in the world. He was like, ‘the tear is next to a nerve’, and I always used to do my right or left soleus.

“He said you’re just as likely to tear your soleus brushing your teeth as you are sprinting.



SUN VEGAS WELCOME OFFER: GET £50 BONUS WHEN YOU JOIN

“I was brushing my teeth on my heels. Again, I never knew when it would come.”

Real Madrid shattered the world transfer record when they lured Bale away from Tottenham for £86million in 2013.

And the lightning-quick winger went the extra mile in an attempt to keep himself on the pitch.



Bale added: “Obviously, people were like, ‘he doesn’t look after himself’. I would literally make sure my calves and soleus were bulletproof. They would be the strongest things ever.

“If it misfires and it goes, there’s nothing I could do about it. I’d have an injection in my back to calm it all down.

“You go through all these things in your career, and it gets to a point where it starts to then go the other way.”

But, after spearheading Wales to their first World Cup since 1958, Bale ran out of reasons to continue pushing through the pain barrier.

He admitted: “It’s like it started to catch up with me. I got to a point where I guess a lot of people do in their career, do you keep going?



“For what reason? What else do I want to achieve? I felt like I achieved everything I wanted to.

“The last thing I did was qualify for a World Cup, which was the one thing that was left on my list.

“I was like, I don’t really want to just keep going for the sake of it. The people always say, do you want football to leave you before you leave football? I felt it was the right time.



“It was a few years prior that I was probably ready. You try and make things stronger and stuff, but it just caught up with me in the end.”

Bale also revealed that his father’s health impacted the decision to hang up his boots.

The Tottenham icon, who notched 71 goals over two spells in North London, confirmed: “With my father’s illness, I wanted to be closer to home and stuff was happening with him.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

“There’s reasons other than I just decided to finish at 33. I understand there’s a long time retired.

“I’ve been planning since I was probably 26 or 27. I know there’s not a long career and you have a long life after.”

Football.title: England World Cup-winning legend to headline luxury cruise and spill beans on Three Lions’ secrets
Football.content:
ENGLAND legend and World Cup winner Sir Geoff Hurst will be headlining a cruise ahead of the upcoming 2026 tournament in North America.

Hurst, 84, will be in attendance to meet and greet guests on the luxury cruise, and ask questions about his iconic England team.





The attacker famously netted a hat-trick in England’s 4-2 win over West Germany in the final of the 1966 World Cup, the only person to do so in a final at the tournament until Kylian Mbappe in 2022.

And now it has been announced that the giant of the English game will join up with P&O Cruises later this month.

He will be sailing on part of Britannia’s route from Barbados to Southampton, joining up with the ship in Portugal on March 30.

He will run four straight nights of Q&A sessions with holidaymakers, looking back to 1966 and forward to the latest edition of the tournament just months away.



The 14-night total cruise will set back vacationers £1,099 and upwards.

Hurst said of his participation: “I’ve been fortunate to experience some extraordinary moments in football, and I’ve always enjoyed sharing those memories with fans.

“To do so on board Britannia, with people who are relaxing on holiday and having a great time, is something I’m really looking forward to.

“The 1966 World Cup was such a momentous occasion for the country, and it’s always a pleasure to relive those memories and answer questions from supporters.



SUN VEGAS WELCOME OFFER: GET £50 BONUS WHEN YOU JOIN

He also gave a positive outlook for England’s chances this summer under manager Thomas Tuchel.

Hurst added: “In sport, as in life, you have to be hopeful and I truly believe England has a very good chance this summer.

“I think Tuchel is developing a disciplined team that will be a dangerous opponent for any side – and I’ve kept the World Cup final date clear in my diary just in case.”

Beyond his time with England, Hurst is also a West Ham legend, making almost 500 appearances for the club.



P&O Cruises president Paul Ludlow added: “Welcoming Sir Geoff Hurst on Britannia is a real coup for us and a very special moment for our guests.

“He is a big part of our nation’s football heritage and a national treasure whose achievements continue to inspire generations of football fans.”

Football.title: Michael Carrick’s Man Utd honeymoon is officially over as alarming ‘midweek slump’ threatens chances of permanent job
Football.content:
THE writing has been on the wall for Michael Carrick.

Carrick tasted defeat for the first time in 11 games as Manchester United head coach across two spells.





The former United midfield general grew up watching Newcastle on the Gallowgate End, and warned his players the stadium was “a tough place to go” before a ball had been kicked.

But these United players are well aware of how challenging a trip to the Toon can be.

United have not won at St James’ Park since a behind-closed-doors clash in October 2020 during Covid restrictions.

Since then it’s been four straight defeats after a draw in 2021.



Carrick’s side were minutes away from what would have been a respectable and hard-fought draw – even if the home side did have ten men.

It would have been a great point on the road for the club’s Champions League push – even after Jacob Ramsey’s red card in first-half stoppage time.

Newcastle outplayed Liverpool with ten-men at the start of the season and United’s recent form suggests that rolling over Eddie Howe’s diminished side was never on the cards.

The Red Devils were billed as favourites, yet keener eyes would have expected less.



BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

Performances have been dwindling since the 1-1 draw at West Ham on February 10, when Benjamin Sesko’s stunning injury-time leveller spared Carrick’s blushes.

That stalemate also came on a midweek night, so it is clear United are struggling to play twice in one week with no European football or cup competitions left this season.

Thirteen days after that West Ham shocker came the narrow 1-0 win at Everton, courtesy of another Sesko winner from the bench following a vintage counter-attacking move.

In both of those games United looked drab on the ball, as the first halves were dampened by poor passing and sluggish movements..



And then came the Crystal Palace win, which saw United offer perhaps their worst opening half under Carrick.

Palace characteristically dropped off after half-time, having played in the Europa League with a small squad three days prior and Maxence Lacroix’s red card turned the direction of the game.

Casemiro, who has never won at Newcastle, and Kobbie Mainoo, 20, have lost the midfield battle in the three games since that Tuesday night at West Ham.

Matheus Cunha looks out of place as a left-winger, Bryan Mbeumo and Amad have both dropped off the ball since Afcon and the full-backs lack any end product.

Carrick's results at Man Utd Man Utd 2-0 Man City Arsenal 2-3 Man Utd Man Utd 3-2 Fulham Man Utd 2-0 Tottenham West Ham 1-1 Man Utd Everton 0-1 Man Utd Man Utd 2-1 Crystal Palace Newcastle 2-1 Man Utd

There have been questions over whether Carrick’s late results after moments of magic, with nine goals scored in the second-half, were sustainable.



Meanwhile, United have won just three out of 10 midweek games this season.

It’s also worth noting that only one of those victories was a true quick-turnaround fixture as the Everton game came after a mini winter break for Carrick’s men.

They face three more non-weekend tests before the campaign ends.

The Red Devils have benefited from FIVE red cards dished out to opponents this season, with Newcastle adding to a long list that includes Chelsea, Everton, Spurs and Palace.

It was the second time United were defeated by ten-men, with the 1-0 loss to the Toffees in November the beginning of the end of fans’ tolerance for Ruben Amorim.



While Casemiro made it 10 v 10 against Chelsea in October, Cristian Romero’s dismissal last month and Lacroix’s banishment played a huge role in United’s wins on those days.

In order for Carrick to get the job in the summer, he must find a way to bounce back when United host Champions League rivals Aston Villa.

The later games against fellow top-five chasers Chelsea and Liverpool have officially crossed into must-win territory.

United have, however, stood up against big names, having picked up the most points against the traditional top-six this season.

It’s not all negative. United have recovered more points (nine) from losing positions than any other side this season.



Even Wes Brown is saying it.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

He told SunSport this week that while the performances are far from glamorous, Carrick’s side have shown resilience to stay in the fight.

They have nine more battles to go.

Football.title: Nottingham Forest legend John Robertson left six-figure sum to his wife & daughter after death on Christmas Day aged 72
Football.content:
FOOTBALL legend John Robertson left £170,000 to his family after his death last year, The Sun can reveal.

The European Cup-winning Nottingham Forest hero, who died on Christmas Day 2025 aged 72, passed the cash to widow Sheryl and daughter Elisabeth.





Documents seen by The Sun show his estate was worth £176,250 but reduced to £168,348 after taxes, debts and fees were settled.

He gave 75 per cent of the estate to his wife, and 25 per cent to his daughter Elisabeth.

Robertson’s other kids, Andrew and Mark, are not given anything in the will, and would have only got a slice of the estate if Sheryl had died before her football great husband.

The will, penned in August 2010, was signed off by the High Court earlier this week.

Robertson was one of the greatest players ever to grace the City Ground, inspiring Brian Clough’s Forest to a league title, two European Cups and two League Cups.

He provided the cross from which Trevor Francis scored a legendary diving header in the 1979 European Cup final against Malmo.

A year later, he scored the winner from outside the box as Forest retained the trophy with a 1-0 victory over Hamburg.



He earned 28 caps for Scotland and scored eight goals, one of which came at the 1982 World Cup against New Zealand.

Robertson was born in Lanarkshire, Scotland, and grew up as a Rangers supporter.

He spent 13 years at Forest before moving to rivals Derby County on a free transfer, only to return after two seasons.

In total, he made over 500 appearances for Forest, bagging 95 goals.

After hanging up his boots as a player he moved into coaching and became part of former team mate Martin O’Neill’s management group at Celtic.



Mourners including Roy Keane, Stuart Pearce and O’Neill attended Robertson’s funeral earlier this year.

Forest paid tribute to their “greatest ever” in a heartfelt statement, which read: “A true great of our Club and a double European Cup winner, John’s unrivalled talent, humility and unwavering devotion to Nottingham Forest will never ever be forgotten.”

Former Celtic striker Chris Sutton, who was coached by Robertson, described him as a mentor.

He said: “Very sad news, John Robertson passing away.

“John was an incredible player, a genius, a legend, a sharp-witted, funny man, a great friend and mentor to me and we will all miss him greatly.”

Football.title: World Cup winner’s wife rushed to hospital after going into labour in stadium stands immediately after goal
Football.content:
ROMA’s exciting 3-3 draw with rivals Juventus on Sunday will forever stay with Paulo Dybala and wife Oriana Sabatini — but not because of the scoreline.

The Argentina star, 32, and his wife welcomed their first child into the world on Monday morning, just hours after the final whistle at the Stadio Olimpico.





Neither of them could have predicted that baby Gia would arrive slightly sooner than expected thanks to the heart-thumping excitement of the Serie A clash.

Dybala was on the bench to watch his side storm into a 3-1 lead thanks to goals from Wesley, Evan Ndicka and on-loan Aston Villa star Donyell Malen.

But the striker wasn’t nearly as invested as his wife, who began having contractions immediately after the third goal hit the back of the net.

Sabatini’s mother Catherine Fulop spoke to Argentinian outlet Teleshow after the birth and revealed the “wild” timeline of events.



She told Teleshow, via Football Italia: “On Sunday, she started having contractions in the stadium, right after the third goal.

“Initially, they were every five minutes.

“Oriana said she wanted to go home and relax in a hot bath, but the contractions were getting more intense.”

“They initially wanted to go to the Gemelli hospital, but it was around 50 minutes away and Ori was already in a lot of pain, so we went to a clinic nearer to us instead at about 3am, as she was already at an advanced stage.”



BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

The birth went smoothly and the couple announced the arrival of their daughter, Gia, on Instagram the day after the match.

The proud parents co-released a snap of the newborn captioned: “A round of applause for mommy and daddy.”

Dybala’s World Cup-winning Argentina team-mates including former Manchester City man Julian Alvarez and Villa star Emi Martinez took to the comments to congratulate the new parents.



Roma went on to drop their two-goal lead, with the match finishing 3-3 to miss the chance to jump into 3rd place.

But the happy couple won’t be dwelling on that as they enjoy their first days with a new member of the family.

Football.title: Premier League star apologises after calling his six-time title-winning manager a ‘s*** coach’
Football.content:
BOURNEMOUTH full-back Alex Jimenez has apologised for calling Massimiliano Allegri a “s*** coach” during his time at AC Milan.

The 20-year-old left the San Siro for the Vitality Stadium in September before his loan move was made permanent last month.





His final weeks in Italy were turbulent, with images on social media appearing to show Jimenez criticising Allegri.

It came after AC Milan finished eighth in the Serie A table at the end of the 2024-25 season, meaning they missed out on a place in Europe.

Images appeared to show Jimenez calling Allegri – who has won six league titles – a “s*** coach” in a text message.

Six months on from the incident, the full-back has apologised and accepted full responsibility.



Speaking to Gianluca Di Marzio and Sky Sports Italy, he said: “It was rubbish, I don’t think Allegri is a ‘sh** coach’ and his history proves that.

“I was coming out of a game, I hadn’t played and I was angry, I chose the wrong person to send that message to.

“From day one, I apologised to Allegri, I didn’t think about what I was writing. He accepted my apology.

“All these situations took away from my sense of serenity, and I was the one who told the club to look for solutions.



CASINO SPECIAL – BEST CASINO BONUSES FROM £10 DEPOSITS

“Milan is a club that I love and I didn’t want to feel bad or negatively impact the team with my bad phase.”

He went on to add: “I don’t think it was ever a problem with the coaches, it was a team problem.

“We needed to have more confidence in ourselves. That’s how it is in football, one year you do badly and the next you do well. Look how it is now.”

Reflecting on his time in Milan, Jimenez said: “It’s the club that launched me in the first team, a team that I loved and still love.



“I had a great time at Milan, I didn’t want to leave.

“But when the season started I realised that I wouldn’t be a key player, there were various situations that I didn’t appreciate.

“I wasn’t given the responsibility I thought I deserved, they needed to believe in me more.

“I’ll give you an example. At the beginning of the season, I asked for the No2 shirt. I wanted it because of the tradition of great full-backs.

“They told me no, that I wasn’t ready and that I was too immature. They were wrong.



“From there, it’s true, I lost concentration and arrived late a few times.”

Since joining from AC Milan at the start of the season, Jimenez has made 26 appearances for Bournemouth, scoring once and providing an assist.

He continued: “It’s a dream for me to play in the Premier League. I’m happy with how things are going at the moment.

“It’s true that the Premier League is more physical than Serie A and LaLiga, but I feel good here. My main quality is my speed and this is the right environment to show that.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

“I grew up at the two biggest clubs in the world. Life is more calm here in Bournemouth, there’s less pressure. The fans are always by our side, for better or for worse.



“My relationship with Andoni Iraola is excellent, I’m very happy to have a leader like him and I hope to play under him for a long time.”

Football.title: Man Utd 'make complaint' to Premier League after raging at 'frustrating' fixture decision
Football.content:
Man Utd 'make complaint' to Premier League after raging at 'frustrating' fixture decision

Manchester United have been handed a succession of 8pm kick-offs in the latest round of fixtures announced by the Premier League, with huge gaps between games also a frustration

View 2 Images Manchester United are annoyed by the latest fixture announcement (Image: George Wood/Getty Images)

Manchester United have reportedly complained to the Premier League and Sky Sports after being left frustrated with the latest fixture announcement. Unlike many of their rivals, United are solely focused on the Premier League - and the latest scheduling has left them far from pleased.

‌



Michael Carrick's side have been scheduled to play three times at 8pm in April, facing Leeds and Brentford on Monday nights at Old Trafford, while travelling to face Chelsea on a Saturday at the same time. It means that 14 of their 34 league games that have been scheduled so far have been 8pm kick-offs.

‌



Those times aren't particularly friendly for fans, particularly for the upcoming trip to Stamford Bridge on April 18. But there are other irritations for the club too.

‌



The Sun reports that club officials are 'fuming' over the frustrating decision by the league and broadcasters. They reportedly understand that United are an 'attractive option' for TV but have still raised the issue with both the Premier League and Sky Sports.

FOLLOW OUR MAN UTD FB PAGE! Latest United news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Michael Carrick gives spiky interview as Man Utd boss left 'hurt' by Newcastle defeat READ MORE :

Paul Scholes rips into Man Utd aces with Michael Carrick jibe minutes after Newcastle loss READ MORE :

The scheduling is affected by United's lack of involvement in other competitions, having been knocked out of the Carabao Cup by Grimsby Town in August and out of the FA Cup in the third round by Brighton in January. Those failures, coupled with no European football, means they are more likely to be moved around by the Premier League for scheduling.

‌



United finished 15th last season and then lost the Europa League final against Tottenham, leaving them short of games to play this season. By contrast, there are nine Premier League sides still taking part in the Champions League, Europa League or Conference League, leaving schedulers with many restrictions.

Carrick's side also have a huge break between matches, with 24 days between their trip to Bournemouth, for another 8pm kick-off on March 20, and the following league game against Leeds on April 13. That is due to the international break and the FA Cup quarter-finals, which United are not involved in.

View 2 Images Michael Carrick's side are annoyed by the fixtures (Image: 2026 Getty Images)

‌



After losing 2-1 against Newcastle on Wednesday night, there is now an 11-day gap before hosting Aston Villa on March 15. United can hardly moan about such quirks, which is because they are not in the FA Cup, which takes centre stage this weekend for the fifth round.

In fact, it can be argued that the lack of involvement in other competitions has given United an edge on their rivals for a top-four finish in the Premier League, given the rest of the top six are all fighting on other fronts.

Carrick suffered his first defeat since becoming interim boss on Wednesday night, with William Osula producing a stunning winner for 10-man Newcastle. Nevertheless, with Villa losing 4-1 to Chelsea, United stayed in third place with nine league games left to play.

Football.title: World Cup star caught up in war forced to flee Iran after bomb exploded 'next to him'
Football.content:
World Cup star caught up in war forced to flee Iran after bomb exploded 'next to him'

A World Cup star gave a harrowing account of his forced exit from Iran following the outbreak of their conflict with the United States and Israel

View 3 Images Duckens Nazon opened up about his forced departure from Iran (Image: Icon Sportswire via Getty Images)

A World Cup star set to play in the summer tournament witnessed a bomb explosion next to him when fleeing Iran. The Middle Eastern country was plunged into war after being subject to joint attacks by Israel and the United States.

‌



Over 1,000 people have died in the conflict according to the Human Rights Activists News Agency, while many others have fled the country looking for refuge. Among those who departed Iran amid the bombings is Haiti's all-time top scorer, Duckens Nazon.

‌



The 31-year-old striker, who plays for Iranian top-flight club Esteghlal, only joined the club last summer but has since returned home to France, where he was born, after fleeing Tehran shortly after the bombings began.

‌



He spoke to RMC radio about his harrowing experience, explaining: "I'm fine, but I'm very tired and shaken by this very eventful journey. I left two and a half or three days ago (he arrived in France on Tuesday morning).

"I left the capital just as the bombings started. I was already on the plane, ready to take off for Istanbul. We had to disembark the plane urgently, and we were told that the airspace was closed because the bombings had begun."

World Cup playoff plunged into doubt by Iran war as nation release worrying statement READ MORE :

USA football chief breaks silence on Iran World Cup boycott threat READ MORE :

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

‌



Alongside former Barcelona star Munir El Haddadi in their departure from Tehran, it was during a taxi journey back to the city that they witnessed a missile explosion next to them. Nazon explained: "Esteghlal's head of security took a taxi to drive us back to Tehran.

"And on the way there, we saw a bombing right next to us. The missile had just exploded nearby, but I thought to myself that there might not be another one in the next minute. It was truly a war zone.

View 3 Images Nazon fled the Iran bombings and managed to return to France (Image: AFP via Getty Images)

‌



"To the right, there was smoke, and to the left, the entire highway was jammed because people were leaving Tehran while we were trying to get in. For us, traffic flowed smoothly, but for them, it was blocked for miles."

He soon made his way towards Azerbaijan in a minibus but was turned down by border guards, with Nazon fearing he'd be forced to make a return to Tehran. However, after intervention from the French ambassador to Azerbaijan, the striker managed to arrive in Baku and subsequently France.

Unaware of when he'll be able to return to Esteghal, the Haiti international admitted he's not had any contact with his Iranian team-mates who've remained in the country. "I haven't heard anything because there's no internet at the club anymore," Nazon divulged.

‌



View 3 Images The striker will compete in the World Cup this summer with Haiti (Image: PA)

"Except for the head of security who received [my message] but didn't reply. I know Munir El Haddadi is in Madrid; he was able to get back before me.

"Moussa Djenepo (the Malian international) is really like a brother to me, and it broke my heart when we said goodbye and he left in a van for Turkey while I was leaving for Azerbaijan. Honestly, it broke my heart because I knew he didn't have a visa either."

Article continues below

Nazon is expected to make the trip to North America this summer for the World Cup for only Haiti's second ever participation in the tournament. They are set to face Brazil, Morocco and Scotland in Group C.

Football.title: Man Utd manager told 'he's had his time' moments before big decision was made
Football.content:
Man Utd manager told 'he's had his time' moments before big decision was made

Manchester United made a big decision regarding their manager after a pundit's comments

View 2 Images Erik ten Hag was sacked by Manchester United after a pundit's brutal comments (Image: Getty Images)

Former Manchester United manager Erik ten Hag was sacked by the club just hours after a pundit called for his dismissal. Ten Hag was relieved of his duties at Old Trafford in October 2024 after a 2-1 defeat by West Ham in the Premier League.

‌



His two-year tenure came to an end after the result at the London Stadium marked United's fourth defeat in nine league matches. The Dutchman’s future had been up in the air for much of 2024 due to a disappointing season before the club’s Ineos bosses decided to extend his deal by two years at the end of the 2024/25 campaign, owing to Ten Hag’s FA Cup triumph.

‌



However, minority owner Sir Jim Ratcliffe decided to end Ten Hag’s tenure after a string of poor results, deeming the situation untenable. Pundit Jamie O’Hara had already urged the United hierarchy to cut the cord on the manager, believing he had been given enough time already.

‌



FOLLOW OUR MAN UTD FB PAGE! Latest United news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

The former Tottenham midfielder said on Sky Sports after the West Ham defeat: "I'd get rid of him; I would have gotten rid of him three months ago. I wouldn't have given him a contract after the FA Cup, I'd have said, 'Thanks very much, you've finished with a trophy, go out on a high.'"

The pundit's prediction hit the mark. Less than a day later, Ten Hag was dismissed by United, as confirmed in their official statement. The announcement read: "We are grateful to Erik for everything he has done during his time with us and wish him well for the future.

‌



Michael Carrick gives spiky interview as Man Utd boss left 'hurt' by Newcastle defeat READ MORE :

Paul Scholes rips into Man Utd aces with Michael Carrick jibe minutes after Newcastle loss READ MORE :

"Ruud van Nistelrooy will take charge of the team as interim head coach, supported by the current coaching team, whilst a permanent head coach is recruited."

Ruben Amorim was brought in as his permanent replacement after two weeks under Van Nistelrooy's leadership. Ten Hag waited until May 2025 to return to management with Bayer Leverkusen, succeeding the highly successful Xabi Alonso.

Yet, Ten Hag’s time in Germany quickly turned ugly, and he was sacked after just two Bundesliga games in charge on September 1, 2025. He is set to work as the technical director of Eredivisie club Twente at the start of the 2026/27 season.

‌



Meanwhile, United fared no better under Amorim, who finished in the bottom half of the table and lost the Europa League final in his first season in charge. The Portuguese head coach’s time at Old Trafford saw him refuse to deviate from his preferred 3-4-2-1 formation and fall out with the club hierarchy, which saw him dismissed this January.

View 2 Images Michael Carrick tasted defeat for the first time as interim Manchester United boss (Image: George Wood, Getty Images)

Since then, Michael Carrick has taken over the reins as interim head coach until the end of the season. The former United midfielder enjoyed a stellar start by winning six and drawing one of his first seven games in charge.

‌



However, his extraordinary run came to an end on Wednesday as United suffered a 2-1 defeat at Newcastle. Amid speculation over whether Carrick’s impressive run would secure him a long-term contract at the end of the season, it remains to be seen whether the Red Devils will bounce back from their recent disappointment.

Another United legend and pundit, Paul Scholes, already made a mocking remark about Carrick, potentially foreshadowing the media frenzy the manager will endure - much like Ten Hag - if there are more losses down the line.

Scholes wrote on his Instagram story: "Michael has definitely got something special about him... cos Utd have been c*** last four games... night."

Article continues below

Football.title: Michael Carrick has given Sir Jim Ratcliffe his answer on being Man Utd's next boss
Football.content:
Michael Carrick has given Sir Jim Ratcliffe his answer on being Man Utd's next boss

Manchester United cannot afford to lean on nostalgia and familiarity - hence why Michael Carrick should not land the manager's job on a permanent basis

View 3 Images Michael Carrick remains one of the favourites to land the Man United job on a permanent basis - despite Wednesday's defeat by Newcastle (Image: Getty Images)

For a few fleeting, deeply confusing weeks, Manchester United had managed to convince themselves that the answer to their deep-rooted structural decay was to hire the first polite, familiar face they came across.

‌



Michael Carrick's odd, friction-free ascent from failed Middlesbrough boss to the anointed saviour of Old Trafford feels very Ole Gunnar Solskjaer-y. His short interim reign has been a triumph of vibes: a period of calm and an application of gentle, soothing nostalgia to an open wound. He stepped into the breach, steadied the ship and brought a kind of quiet, zipped-up dignity back to the club.

‌



But against Newcastle on Wednesday, the fever finally broke, the rose-tinted glasses were unceremoniously knocked off, and the veil was lifted to reveal a stark, unavoidable reality: Carrick just isn't that good.

‌



It seems harsh to pour scorn on a manager who has dragged a seemingly rudderless side who were flirting with relegation last season to the top of the Premier League form table. But look closer at the fabric of United's 'resurgence', and the thread immediately starts to pull.

FOLLOW OUR MAN UTD FB PAGE! Latest United news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Paul Scholes rips into Man Utd aces with Michael Carrick jibe minutes after Newcastle loss READ MORE :

Harry Maguire verdict has been reached by Michael Carrick with Man Utd to make decision READ MORE :

The truth is, United's results over the past few weeks have been entirely reliant on a highly specific, wildly unsustainable holy trinity of footballing fortune: the ephemeral new manager bounce, an epidemic of opposition indiscipline, and more last-minute strikes than National Rail.

‌



Let's take those structural crutches one by one. Firstly, the 'bounce' - that peculiar, well-documented psychological reset where players temporarily remember how to run and kick footballs properly simply because the man standing in front of the whiteboard has a different voice. Both Carrick and United have undeniably benefited from this atmospheric detox. But it's a sugar rush, not a sustainable diet.

Secondly, the Red Devils have been gorging themselves on the bizarrely consistent willingness of their opponents to self-immolate. Red cards for Tottenham, Crystal Palace and Newcastle have provided cushy numerical advantages that render any need to tactically outfox the opposition redundant.

In fact, over their last five games, United have had an extra man on the field for roughly a third of the total minutes - 140, to be exact - yet still managed to drop points in two of them.

‌



View 3 Images Newcastle's 2-1 win over Man Utd on Wednesday exposed Carrick's frailties (Image: Newcastle United via Getty Image)

And finally, there's the reliance on late goals. Those desperate, breathless, last-gasp bailouts against Arsenal, Fulham and West Ham were unquestionably thrilling, but papered over the cracks of performances that deserved little more than a slow clap.

The 2-1 defeat by Newcastle was the moment all three of these crutches spectacularly splintered. Stripped of the honeymoon period, toothless against 10 men, and finally on the wrong end of a stoppage-time sucker punch, the underlying reality of this United side was laid bare. They are not markedly improved. They are the same structurally disjointed entity, just wearing a slightly more pleasant expression.

‌



Which brings us to the ultimate danger. Carrick is, without a doubt, a fundamentally decent human being. He's steeped in the gallant, almost mystical 'United DNA'. For calming the storm, he deserves a warm handshake and a basket of muffins. But decency is not a tactical philosophy.

If Sir Jim Ratcliffe hands Carrick the keys on a permanent basis, United will be sleepwalking back into the Ole Gunnar Solskjaer vortex. Solskjaer was, of course, the ultimate custodian of good vibes. Much like Carrick, he arrived mid-season as a temporary, soothing balm, only to ride a fleeting, sugary wave of results all the way to a permanent contract.

View 3 Images Man United can't afford to waste years on Carrick like they did on Solskjaer (Image: Getty)

‌



He was a man whose profound understanding of the club's history was foolishly conflated with having the elite, ruthless competence required to manage the Red Devils in the modern era - and United wasted years hoping that nostalgia and nice-guy energy would magically crystallise into a Premier League title. They cannot afford to waste time hoping for the same alchemy with Carrick.

Being a man who - to use the sport's most hollow, interminable cliché - simply, "gets it", isn't enough. United need an elite, cold-blooded tactician. Someone with grit, experience and terrifying substance. Carrick has served as an effective and necessary mood-lifter, but a change in atmosphere doesn't guarantee a change in long-term trajectory.

For once, United must resist the narcotic, warm embrace of the past when things go south, and opt instead for cold, unsentimental, unflinching competence.

Article continues below

Football.title: Arne Slot drops Florian Wirtz return hint as Liverpool boss gives positive injury update
Football.content:
Arne Slot drops Florian Wirtz return hint as Liverpool boss gives positive injury update

Florian Wirtz has missed Liverpool's last two fixtures due to injury but Arne Slot could welcome back his £116million signing when the Reds play Wolves in the FA Cup

View 2 Images Florian Wirtz is closing in on a return (Image: Chris Brunskill/Fantasista/Getty Images)

Liverpool boss Arne Slot has confirmed that Florian Wirtz is back in training and could feature in their FA Cup tie at Wolves. Wirtz, 22, suffered an injury in the warm-up ahead of the Reds' 1-0 win at Nottingham Forest two weeks ago.

‌



The German has been sidelined for Liverpool's last two games and his creativity was sorely missed as Slot's side slipped to an embarrassing 2-1 defeat against Wolves in the Premier League on Tuesday. Coincidentally, Liverpool will return to Molineux on Friday in the FA Cup fifth round and Wirtz is expected to travel.

‌



On Wednesday, the Reds' £116million signing trained with his team-mates for the first time as he stepped up his recovery. Speaking at Thursday's pre-match press conference, Slot confirmed: "He trained half-and-half with the team yesterday and made the next step in his rehab.

‌



"Let's see where he is today. He could be available for a few minutes tomorrow in the best-case scenario. Everyone else is fine."

FOLLOW OUR LIVERPOOL FB PAGE! Latest Reds news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Steven Gerrard becomes the second Liverpool legend to demand Arne Slot make change READ MORE :

Slot reported a clean bill of health from the Wolves game. However, the Reds remain without Alexander Isak, Conor Bradley, Wataru Endo, Giovanni Leoni and Stefan Bajcetic.

‌



Injuries have been a theme of Liverpool's season but Slot maintains that he's happy with how the club recruited last summer. "I'm happy with what we did last summer, we signed so many great players but you can't predict so many would be injured," the Dutchman explained.

"Adaptation you can predict. It's the model we are having that we aren't having 25 players over here. It hurts even more if you have three or four long-term injuries. I knew when I came in this was the model and I'm happy with that."

Will Liverpool get revenge on Wolves? Give us your prediction in the comments section.

‌



View 2 Images Florian Wirtz is back in training (Image: Andrew Powell/Liverpool FC via Getty Images)

Slot has demanded a response from his players after their midweek loss and could ring in the changes. "We have a few [options] but we don't have 11," he added.

"I always have options to bring in and players can bring in and let them start. Not for the first time this season, three days after a game where we have dropped points, I've seen us do quite a lot this season, we have to be better than we were last time."

Football.title: Harry Maguire 'refused to pay £50k bribe' after Greek arrest as Man Utd star makes vow
Football.content:
Harry Maguire 'refused to pay £50k bribe' after Greek arrest as Man Utd star makes vow

Harry Maguire has seen his conviction upheld after his retrial in Greece finally went ahead on Wednesday, as the Manchester United star was handed a 15-month suspended sentence

View 2 Images Harry Maguire plans to appeal the guilty verdict (Image: Robbie Jay Barratt - AMA/Getty Images)

Harry Maguire allegedly refused to pay a £50,000 bribe to make his Greek arrest ordeal 'go away'. Maguire, 33, was cuffed on the Greek island if Mykonos in August 2020 and the retrial over his initial conviction finally took place on Wednesday after four separate postponements.

After fighting to clear his name for the best part of six years, his conviction of assault and bribery was upheld. Originally handed a 21-month suspended sentence in 2020, the Manchester United star has seen it changed to 15 months and 20 days, and been slapped with a €1,500 (£1,304) fine.

Maguire, who has always denied wrongdoing, plans to appeal the guilty verdict. Following the retrial, a report from the Sun has emerged claiming that the England international was repeatedly told by undercover police that if he paid them £50,000, the incident would 'disappear'.

A source told the newspaper that Maguire would rather be found guilty than pay a bribe, and he intends to never pay a single penny. It's added that 'all will be revealed in his upcoming documentary'.

FOLLOW OUR MAN UTD FB PAGE! Latest United news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Maguire played the full match for United on Wednesday, hours after his conviction was upheld. The centre-back was unable to prevent Michael Carrick's side from losing 2-1 to Newcastle at St James' Park in the Premier League.

The incident took place while Maguire, his family and some friends were on holiday in Mykonos following the conclusion of the extended 2019-20 Premier League season, during which he was United's captain. The group - which included his wife, Fern, sister Daisy, brother Joe and five others - were waiting for a taxi to take them back to their villa when two Albanian men approached Daisy and tried to talk to her.

View 2 Images Harry Maguire was initially convicted in 2020 (Image: AFP via Getty Images)

Maguire claimed that the men injected Daisy with an unknown substance before she began drifting in and out of consciousness. The ex-Leicester player said that he attempted to get Daisy to a hospital but was instead taken to a police station, where he alleges that he was beaten on the legs by uniformed men who told him that his football career was over.

One police offer alleged that Harry and Joe had tried to bribe officers. Maguire, who denies the allegation, spent two nights in a police cell before standing trial.

He was found guilty of repeated bodily harm, attempted bribery, violence against public employees and insulting behaviour. Joe and friend Christopher Sharman were also convicted.

Joe has since been acquitted of attempted bribery. The court, however, upheld charges of aggravated assault and verbal abuse of the police.

Football.title: Harry Maguire verdict has been reached by Michael Carrick with Man Utd to make decision
Football.content:
Harry Maguire verdict has been reached by Michael Carrick with Man Utd to make decision

The next step of Harry Maguire's Manchester United career has a likely resolution if Michael Carrick has his way

View 2 Images Michael Carrick has put his faith into Harry Maguire (Image: Shaun Brooks - CameraSport, CameraSport via Getty Images)

Michael Carrick has only praised Harry Maguire's significance to Manchester United amidst uncertainty surrounding his future at Old Trafford. The centre-back is in his seventh campaign at the club but with his contract running out at the end of the season, it could well be his final one.

‌



Maguire - who was this week given a 15-month suspended jail sentence after being found guilty of non-serious assault in Mykonos in 2020 - is reportedly eager to prolong his career in Manchester.

‌



Whilst his position in the squad appeared to be dwindling under Ruben Amorim, given he began just four Premier League fixtures under the Portuguese manager before his January dismissal, he's experienced a revival under Carrick.

‌



Since the interim appointment of the former United star, Maguire has started all eight league matches, with the sole defeat arriving in the 2-1 loss to Newcastle United on Wednesday night.

His return to the starting XI has witnessed the defender deliver some of the finest displays of his Old Trafford career, and he's back in the running for a World Cup place in Thomas Tuchel's England squad.

Michael Carrick gives spiky interview as Man Utd boss left 'hurt' by Newcastle defeat READ MORE :

Paul Scholes rips into Man Utd aces with Michael Carrick jibe minutes after Newcastle loss READ MORE :

FOLLOW OUR MAN UTD FB PAGE! Latest United news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

‌



Whilst it remains uncertain whether Carrick will be granted the permanent head coaching position at United, should he assume the responsibility, a fresh contract for Maguire may feature high on his agenda following his recent remarks about him.

Ahead of United's triumph over Crystal Palace last week, Carrick said: "Harry's an impressive character. He's had a great career so far and hopefully there's a lot more to come. I think in what he's been here and the journey and the experiences that he's had certainly since he's been here and with England as well I think show exactly what he is.

View 2 Images Harry Maguire is in top form at Old Trafford (Image: Visionhaus)

‌



"Within the squad there is that balance, you want that younger potential and excitement, but you need the sweet spot in the middle with players at the peak, and experience is massive. It's hard to put a price on how valuable that can be.

"Harry has gained an awful lot of experience with this club. Casemiro leaving is another one and we have to understand what that means." When questioned whether Maguire is delivering the finest football of his career, Carrick responded: "There is a case [for that].

"As your career goes when you're a young player and everything is fresh, you're fearless and there's not many scars there. You get to your peak years and you feel good and everything is there, but you've had a few ups and downs.

Article continues below

"You get towards the later times and the experience should make you a better player, we've seen that with Harry's performances of late. That's why experience is important, I felt it when I was playing, 31 or 32 were my best years of being in that sweet spot to do it physically still but use the experiences to know what is coming."

After their loss at St James' Park, United won't be back in action until March 15 when they welcome fellow top four contenders Aston Villa, who suffered a heavy 4-1 defeat at home to Chelsea on Wednesday.

Football.title: Newcastle post cheeky Man Utd dig as match-winner comes back to haunt Michael Carrick
Football.content:
Newcastle post cheeky Man Utd dig as match-winner comes back to haunt Michael Carrick

Michael Carrick's Manchester United suffered heartbreak in stoppage time as 10-man Newcastle pulled off an unlikely victory thanks to William Osula's incredible goal

View Image William Osula was the hero for Newcastle (Image: Serena Taylor/Newcastle United via Getty Images)

Newcastle shared a cheeky post mocking Manchester United after William Osula came back to haunt them. Osula, 22, fired Newcastle to an unlikely 2-1 win after his dazzling goal in the 90th minute at St James' Park.

‌



Eddie Howe's side had been reduced to 10 men on the brink of half-time after Jacob Ramsey was controversially sent off for two yellow cards. Despite that, Newcastle took the lead through Anthony Gordon after Bruno Fernandes had given away a penalty.

‌



United captain Fernandes quickly made amends by setting up Casemiro to equalise before the break. In the second half, though, the visitors couldn't break Newcastle down and were eventually punished by Osula.

‌



The Dane picked up the ball on the counter attack before blitzing past Tyrell Malacia. Cutting inside on his left foot, Osula whipped a shot past Harry Maguire which left Senne Lammens rooted and crashed into the back of the net.

FOLLOW OUR MAN UTD FB PAGE! Latest United news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Gareth Bale opens up on bombshell Man Utd transfer talks and confirms 'higher' bid READ MORE :

Michael Carrick gives spiky interview as Man Utd boss left 'hurt' by Newcastle defeat READ MORE :

Some at United should know all about Osula's talent, as back in 2014, he won the MU Soccer Schools World Skills Final. The forward played at Old Trafford aged 11 and impressed enough to win the award.

‌



A post on X showed Osula receiving a shield from United legends Bryan Robson and Gary Pallister. "Congratulations to 11-year-old William Osula from Denmark, winner of today's MU Soccer Schools World Skills Final," it read.

Fast forward just over 11 years and and Osula came back to haunt the Red Devils. Newcastle didn't miss the chance to mock their opponents, resharing the 2014 post and writing: "Time flies."

Did Newcastle deserve to win? Have your say in the comments section.

‌



Content cannot be displayed without consent

The winner was only Osula's second Premier League goal of the season and his first since August after being limited to one start. The forward told TNT Sports post-match: "That was the best feeling ever. I was so happy for that.

"When I came on I just prayed that I was going to score and make something happen and thank God that that happened. I was practicing that in training yesterday, just cut in and finish it, so the hard work paid off."

‌



Howe added: "He did 10 shots at the end of training and he wanted more. Fair play to him. It's the desire to repeat and to practice. He's delivered an amazing moment for the supporters here.

"It's a great moment for him and he deserves it. Hopefully it's turning point for him. A big moment for us. We've proved we're competitive against any team."

Football.title: Raducanu: Roig instigated split - it's hard to block out the noise
Football.content:
Emma Raducanu revealed it was former coach Francisco Roig's decision to part ways and that noise surrounding her coaching appointments has been difficult to block out.

The former US Open champion split with Roig following her second-round defeat at the Australian Open. The Spaniard was Raducanu's ninth coach since her US Open victory in 2021.

Raducanu has since reunited with Mark Petchey on a day-to-day basis during Indian Wells, while continuing to work closely with hitting partner Alexis Canter.

Emma Raducanu's NINE coaches

Nigel Sears



Best known for coaching former top-five players Amanda Coetzer, Daniela Hantuchova, Anett Kontaveit and Ana Ivanovic, Sears joined her team in April 2021 and oversaw her incredible breakthrough run to the Wimbledon fourth round.



Andrew Richardson



Guided Raducanu to her historic Grand Slam title at the US Open as a qualifier. Raducanu opted not to extend his trial contract immediately after the victory.



Torben Beltz



Angelique Kerber's former coach lasted only five months. They split in April 2022 with Raducanu transitioning towards a new training model involving LTA coaching support.



Dmitry Tursunov



Raducanu took on the former pro on a trial basis during the summer of 2022 but Tursunov decided not to continue, later citing red flags in her camp that he felt could not be ignored for a long-term commitment.



Sebastian Sachs



The German joined in late 2022 but the partnership was cut short when Raducanu required multiple surgeries, leading to a mutual split.



Nick Cavaday



The 14-month stint was her longest professional partnership, helping her return to the top 60 before he stepped down in early 2025 due to personal health issues.



Vlado Platenik



Hired on a trial basis in March 2025, the partnership lasted only 14 days.



Mark Petchey



Andy Murray's former coach provided crucial tactical knowledge through Wimbledon where she went out to world No 1 Aryna Sabalenka, despite an encouraging display.



Francisco Roig



Appointed in August 2025, Rafael Nadal's former mentor offered elite tactical clarity, but after her Australian Open exit in January 2026, Raducanu announced their split.



"After Australia, me and Francis were talking. We have a great relationship, we could have a really open and honest conversation," Raducanu told Sky Sports News.

"He ended up saying, 'look, I don't think this is going in the way we both want it to' so he ended it really.

"In a way I think we had a few moments where we weren't agreeing on a few things. Other than that we still maintain a great relationship and I've seen him here, it's been great to a see a familiar, great face around."

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Tim Henman, Laura Robson and Naomi Cavaday discuss how much impact coach Mark Petchey will have as the two reunite ahead of Indian Wells

Petchey had already been scheduled to be in Indian Wells for commentary duties, with Raducanu reintroducing his support after the pair's successful spell on a temporary basis together last year.

He teamed up with Raducanu in Miami as she reached the quarter-finals last March, before remaining present as she made it to the fourth round in Rome before performing well at Wimbledon, where she went toe-to-toe with Aryna Sabalenka in a glimpse at a return to top form.

"Mark was going to be here anyway for commentating and I've been working with Alexis who has been helping me so much," she said.

"With Mark I knew he'd be in Indian Wells so I asked him to come out a few days earlier just to do some stuff with me on the court and try to feel back in a better way with my game.

"At the start of the year I didn't feel too good but the last few days I've been feeling better. It's not something that has really been organised going forward but I knew he would be here and it's been great, I always love being on court with him."

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Jonathan Overend believes that if Raducanu can find the right balance with the correct coach, she will be set up for a great year of tennis

A carousel of coaching changes has prompted a wave of outside noise surrounding Raducanu in recent years, coupling with a torrid time with multiple injury setbacks amid her bid to unearth her best tennis.

The British No 1 recently suffered a first-round defeat to Antonia Ruzic at the Dubai Tennis Championships, having retired due to illness against Camila Osorio in the first round in Qatar.

"It is a challenge because I think it affects some of the decisions I make," she said of outside noise. "I don't want to start working with anyone not knowing if it's going to be 100 per cent set in stone, because I feel like regardless of how or why it ends it will be put on me, even if I didn't end it with Francis.

"It does affect at some points but I know at the end of the day I need to take the decision that's going to make me the best player. By having the experience of spending too long doing something I'm not comfortable doing, it's only effected me and made me feel worse about my tennis.

"Having gone through that, I don't want to because I know how it makes me feel on the court."

Raducanu returns to action on Friday when she faces Anastasia Zakharova following a first-round bye.

Football.title: Lingfield and Newcastle feature on Thursday
Football.content:
It's a busy day of action on Sky Sports Racing, with action to come from Lingfield and Newcastle.

6.30 Newcastle - Doctor Khan Junior fancied to go close

Doctor Khan Junior tops a field of eight for the feature Win £250,000 With BetMGM's Golden Goals Handicap at Newcastle.

George Oldroyd's seven-year-old battled bravely to land a sixth all-weather success at Southwell in January, and he ought to give another bold bid in this similar event with PJ McDonald retaining the ride.

Last year's winner Nikovo has filled the frame on his last three starts including when third at Kempton last time and given that form has worked out well, he can go well from the same mark under Jason Hart.

Aisling Oscar is back again after suffering defeat the last twice after his record-equalling run, while Eldrickjones cannot be dismissed having been only beaten three lengths when fourth the last day.

3.25 Lingfield - Fidelius and Toolatetonegotiate headline

Fidelius and Toolatetonegotiate headline a trappy rendition of this Make The Move To Midnite Handicap at Lingfield.

Robert Cowell and Tom Marquand team up with Fidelius, who landed a course and distance handicap on his penultimate run before chasing home Atomic Force at Chelmsford. That was a Class 2 heat, and this looks an excellent opportunity for him to return to winning ways.

Toolatetonegotiate has a length to find with Fidelius from their battle here in February and could get closer with a sharper break from the stalls.

The Thames Boatman rates best of the rest, having struck gold over this course and distance three starts back. He remains 2lbs higher than that winning mark, but isn't without a chance.

2.34 Saint-Cloud - Silius and Ultrafragola face returning Fast Tracker

The Listed Prix Altipan takes centre stage at Saint-Cloud, with Silius and Ultrafragola likely types.

Francis-Henri Graffard conquered all before him last season and he saddles Ultrafragola with Christophe Soumillon in the plate. She doubled her career tally when landing a conditions race at Deauville on her reappearance last month and deserves a crack at this level.

Fast Tracker was a useful three-year-old when trained by Henri-Alex Pantall and has not been seen since scoring by six lengths at Deauville in this grade back in October 2024. He makes his return from 509 days off course, has undergone a gelding operation and debuts for Ed Walker.

Silius has a good win to runs record and having been successful at Chantilly on his last start in January, he merits plenty of respect.

Football.title: Hamilton: My goal is to win 2026 F1 title
Football.content:
Lewis Hamilton says his goal is to win the 2026 Formula 1 title and Ferrari are "leaving behind the bad" as he begins his second campaign with the team.

Hamilton endured a frustrating first season at Ferrari in 2025, failing to stand on the podium all year for the first time in his F1 career, but has visibly been in a more buoyant mood leading up to this weekend's season-opening Australian Grand Prix.

Not since 2021 has Hamilton been able to challenge for the championship and the 41-year-old stressed he is "much happier" compared to 12 months ago having spent a year with Ferrari to understand their culture and team operations.

"We got great mileage done in winter testing - an amazing amount of work has been done from the team back at the factory, but also delivering on those tests," he said.

"We've learned a lot from last year. We're leaving behind the bad and moving forwards with the good, and we're just sharp. We are prepared, and we know what we need to do. We also know there's massive challenges for all of us with the new rules and regulations."

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player The Sky Sports F1 team give their bold predictions for the 2026 season.

Ferrari impressed during Bahrain testing as Charles Leclerc was quickest with both Hamilton and Leclerc also showing strong long run pace - which gives a more representative view of the pecking order.

The paddock consensus is Ferrari and Mercedes are favourites for the first race in Melbourne, although Hamilton hinted Red Bull may have been sandbagging when asked about his realistic ambitions.

He said: "The goal is to win. That's what everyone's working towards - every team is. But that's our goal, to maximise on every opportunity, to be lean, to be hopefully fighting in the top group, hopefully in the first races. We don't really know.

"Mercedes looks particularly quick, and I'm not really sure whether we have seen the full unleashed Red Bull yet, so it's really, really exciting.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player McLaren's Oscar Piastri was left 'stranded' on the track before Lewis Hamilton once again aced the practice start in his Ferrari during Bahrain testing

"But I do know that, whatever the case, I feel like I've got a great group of people behind me who are head down, focused on bringing performance and really maximising from every weekend."

Hamilton explains how 'I lost sight of who I was'

Hamilton struggled in F1's ground effect era, which began in 2022, but the new 2026 cars should suit the Ferrari driver's style of being able to handle a car that can slide more while being under control.

The Ferrari driver wrote in an Instagram post that there will be "no holding back" and stated last month he "forgot who I was".

Asked where the renewed energy has come from, he said: "A combination of things. The break was really positive. It was my surroundings, it was the people that I was with. It's not my first rodeo, so it's understanding how to flip things, and it's not that easy to do each time.

"But I always talk about cultivating a positive mental attitude, and that's what I focus on my winter doing, and a lot of it came from training. I was training hard from Christmas Day.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player As Formula 1 enters a new era, Tate McRae’s GRAMMY-nominated Just Keep Watching will become the new title track for Sky Sports’ F1 coverage this season

"Also knowing that I believe in myself, that I put more work in than anyone around me, and I believe in what my myself and rediscovering myself was really a big part of it as well. I kind of like lost sight for a second of who I was, and that person's gone, so you won't see that person again."

Hamilton parted ways with his long-term manager Mark Hynes during the off-season and is yet to find a new race engineer after Riccardo Adami was moved into a different role at Ferrari. Carlo Santi, Kimi Raikkonen's former engineer, will work with Hamilton until an appointment is made.

Hamilton continued: "[I've] come back with that positive mental frame of mind, so I feel great arriving here. Training has been fantastic, the engine, the work with the team has been amazing.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Former racing driver Anthony Davidson believes if Ferrari's Lewis Hamilton can manage qualifying, he could compete for his eighth world title

"Changes within my own personal space and in how I interact with the team, how the team is working is so much smoother than it was last year."

Thursday March 5

9.45pm: F3 Practice

10.55pm: F2 Practice

Friday March 6

Football.title: 'Like being electrocuted!' – Aston Martin reveal shocking extent of vibration issues
Football.content:
Aston Martin chief Adrian Newey has suggested it's unlikely that either of the team's cars will be able to finish Sunday's season-opening Australian Grand Prix due to concerns that vibrations could cause serious damage to their drivers.

It had become clear during last month's two pre-season testing events in Bahrain that the team were experiencing major issues with their Honda power unit, the first supplied to them in a new partnership for 2026 with the Japanese manufacturer.

The extent of the issues were laid bare on Thursday morning as Newey held a press conference with Honda executive Koji Watanabe in Melbourne, at which they revealed the power unit's vibration problem was not only impacting the functionality of the car but also the safety of drivers Fernando Alonso and Lance Stroll in Sunday's 58-lap race.

Newey said: "Fernando is of the feeling that he can't do more than 25 laps consecutively before he will risk permanent nerve damage to his hands. Lance is of the opinion that he can't do more than 15 laps before that threshold.

"I think there's no point in (not) being open and honest in this meeting (press conference) on our expectations. It's something that unfortunately Koji and I haven't had a chance to discuss properly prior to this meeting.

Image: Alonso says he has experienced numbness from the vibrations of the Aston Martin

"We are going to have to be very heavily restricted on how many laps we do in the race until we get on top of the source of the vibration and improve the vibration at source."

Along with concern for the drivers, Watanabe confirmed that Honda are also unable to run the power unit at full capacity due to the vibration issue.

Watanabe said: "During the Bahrain pre-season test, we experienced unexpected vibration, which caused damage to battery-related components of the power unit. As a result, we were unable to complete the mileage we had originally targeted.

"The engineers from Honda and Aston Martin are working closely as one team to develop and evaluate the multiple countermeasures to issue.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Lance Stroll spins off the track after a lock-up and brings out the red flag!

"Based on extensive testing, we will introduce countermeasures, what we believe to be the most effective solution at this stage, starting this week. However, its effectiveness cannot yet be fully guaranteed under the real track condition. So certain conditions will be applied to power unit operation this week.

"Honda and Aston Martin will continue working closely together as one team, and further measures are already under consideration. So, we are not able to share the technical details, so we ask for your patience as we continue working towards unlocking our full performance potential."

Given that Formula 1 has introduced completely new power units for the first time since 2014, with a shift towards more electrical power as the sport attempts to become more sustainable, there were expected to be teething issues.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Ted Kravitz explains why Aston Martin fears their engine vibrations could cause 'permanent nerve damage', which could prevent Fernando Alonso and Lance Stroll from finishing the race in Australia.

However, it is a major shock that Honda, who powered Red Bull's Max Verstappen to four successive championships from 2021-2024, have got it so badly wrong, while first-time engine manufacturers Red Bull and Audi appear to be competitive.

Watanabe said he was unable to give a timeline for how long it will take Honda to get their power unit to a competitive level, as he made the concerning admission that they have yet to find the "root cause" of the issue.

He added: "Of course, I want to hurry up, but at this moment, it's quite difficult to say when and how."

Despite what appears to be a dire situation for Aston Martin during Newey's first season in charge, he insisted that the team retain faith in their engine supplier.

He said: "Do I believe in our partners, in Honda's ability to bring that power up and to be competitive? Absolutely. They have a proven track record and we have total faith."

Stroll makes 'electrocution' comparison | Alonso explains 'numb' feeling

While both Alonso and Stroll played down the level of pain the vibrations were causing them, their attempts to explain the sensations they experienced in the cockpit during testing reiterated the seriousness of the situation.

Stroll said: "I guess just like electrocute yourself in a chair or something like that, and it's not far off. It's a very uncomfortable vibration. It's bad for the engine but it's also bad for the human inside the car. We need to just get on top of it but I think we will."

Alonso added: "The driver, we feel those as well and feel a little bit numb in the fingers and feet as well because of this low frequency all the time in the car."

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player A red flag was issued during day two of the Bahrain Test after Alonso's Aston Martin stopped on the track.

Despite Newey setting low expectations for Sunday's race, Alonso expressed hope that a solution found since Bahrain could enable him to make it to the chequered flag in Melbourne.

"I hope to finish the race, first of all," he said. "It's true that in Bahrain and Barcelona we faced these anomalous vibrations coming from the engine. I think they are understood. There were a couple of solutions on the dyno since Bahrain, so hopefully we will have a better feeling in FP1.

"I'm optimistic that after the changes they made the last week, we can have a more normal weekend. If we can finish the race unexpectedly, that would be a good first race of the year."

Stroll added: "It all depends how much we've improved since Bahrain. If we still have the same vibration issues we had with the car in Bahrain, it's going to be hard to do much more than 10-15 laps.

"If we resolved those issues the last couple of weeks, then I think we can go much further into the race. Time will tell."

Football.title: Russell pinpoints Verstappen, Leclerc as main F1 title rivals
Football.content:
George Russell has pinpointed Max Verstappen and Charles Leclerc as Mercedes' main title threats ahead of the 2026 Formula 1 season.

Russell is the bookmakers' favourite for the title and by Sky Sports F1's pundits given Mercedes' form in pre-season testing and their dominance following the last change in the power unit regulations in 2014.

Although Ferrari set the quickest lap times in Bahrain testing, Mercedes were very fast on the long runs, which are more representative, and the Silver Arrows did not appear to show their full hand over one lap.

Asked who he saw as Mercedes' biggest title challengers when it comes to the Drivers' Championship, Russell said to the media: "I would say Max and Leclerc."

His team-mate Kimi Antonelli added: "We think Red Bull is very strong, so for sure Max is going to be in the mix. Ferrari look very strong in Bahrain, both in long runs and on a single lap, so especially Leclerc, but I wouldn't write off Lewis as well, because he can be very much in the mix.

"Maybe also the McLaren, but they seem a little bit more on the back foot compared to Ferrari and Red Bull, but for sure, Max and Charles could be the main ones."

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player George Russell targets Max Verstappen in 2026, saying the Red Bull driver will be in the fight this season

However, Russell appeared to soften his stance when speaking later on media day to Sky Sports F1 when discussing title contenders.

"Red Bull have exceeded everybody's expectations with their engine, so naturally Max will be in the fight. Ferrari also look very strong," he said.

"Charles looks very strong but you can never discount Lewis [Hamilton]. As it stands, those are the other drivers that are on the radar, who I think will be in the mix.

"McLaren have been so successful in years gone by. They look a bit on the back foot currently, but that can all change. We saw that a couple of years ago when they went from the back to the front, so we can't discount anyone."

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Lewis Hamilton says he's much happier in his second year with Ferrari and lays out his target to win the 2026 title

Russell is yet to mount an F1 championship challenge after four seasons at Mercedes where they were unable to provide him a car that could consistently win races.

The 28-year-old says this year is a "huge opportunity" but he's in a "good place" and "ready to fight" his rivals.

"I'm going about it the same as everyone in the season, which is to get the absolute maximum from the package," he said.

"I think we have delivered a strong car at the moment. However, we're not taking anything for granted because every time there is a new set of regulations, the rate of development is so strong.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Martin Brundle believes George Russell has the confidence to compete for the title if his car allows it

"We've seen that, even in 2022, Ferrari were the team to beat in the first four or five races, then by the end of the season, they weren't even close to a championship fight.

"We've got to be very diligent. And even if we do start the season very well, it doesn't really count for a lot. So, we need to keep on pushing and just taking it race by race."

Russell: Red Bull 'suspiciously slow' in second Bahrain test

Russell already expressed his wariness over Red Bull's pace in pre-season testing, stating they had "over half a second" of an advantage on energy deployment in the first Bahrain test. After the second test, he said the "scary" advantage had been "closed drastically".

Energy deployment will be critical this year due to the new power unit regulations where the output is split into 50 per cent internal combustion engine and 50 per cent electrical.

He said of Red Bull in Melbourne: "From our side, there hasn't really been any strategic poker. We've been very consistent with our run plans over the course of the two weeks, staying pretty consistent on fuel loads and power stuff.

"We didn't change our plans during the course of the test based on us looking strong or slow. I think Red Bull looked suspiciously slow in the second test.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Max Verstappen says he's not overcomplicating the Red Bull, joking if he had to race a 'shopping trolley', he'd drive it to the limit

"We had them down as, I believe, the quickest in the first test, and based on our numbers, they went seven tenths slower compared to themselves in the second test, whereas ourselves and Ferrari went a couple of tenths quicker with a few new upgrades to the car, and as you're progressing, so I'm struggling to wrap my head around how they've lost seven tenths in a week.

"I expect them to be very strong, to be honest, and that's why we're just all really intrigued to see how it pans out."

Red Bull have built their own Red Bull powertrains engine in collaboration with Ford and it was expected the new project would see them behind in performance compared to Ferrari and Mercedes.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player The most fiery moments in Formula 1 that happened outside the car

But, the Milton Keynes-based outfit were largely reliable and fast in testing considering the expectations but Verstappen did not want to be drawn into any comparisons with the other top teams.

"I'm very happy with what we did in pre-season. It was a great and proud moment for everyone - how the whole project come together between the engine and the car," he said.

"I was positively surprised with how everything felt. The rule changes have been really complex but in terms of the feeling in the car and the driving experience it was good.

"Looking at the performance side of things, we want to be a little bit faster. But from the things I think we learned, we were not the quickest, but I have no idea [about how Red Bull stack up]."
